name: Presidential plane shot down
desc: The aircraft carrying President Habyarimana was struck by missiles on approach to Kigali International Airport. All aboard perished. Within hours, organized mass killings began.

name: PM Agathe Uwilingiyimana killed
desc: Prime Minister Agathe Uwilingiyimana was killed at her home by Presidential Guard members, eliminating the constitutional succession.

name: 10 Belgian UNAMIR peacekeepers killed
desc: Ten Belgian soldiers assigned to protect the PM were disarmed, tortured, and murdered at Camp Kigali. This was calculated to provoke Belgium's withdrawal from UNAMIR.

name: Kigali roadblock killings begin
desc: Roadblocks manned by Interahamwe and military were established across Kigali. Identity cards were checked and Tutsi civilians were systematically killed. Thousands perished in the first days.

name: Kabuga area massacres
desc: East of Kigali center, large-scale killings erupted in the Kabuga area as militia moved neighborhood to neighborhood conducting house-to-house searches.

name: Runda killings
desc: The Runda area between central Kigali and Kabuga saw intense violence as fleeing civilians were intercepted at roadblocks and gathering points.

name: ETO Kicukiro — peacekeepers withdraw
desc: Approximately 4,000 civilians at the ETO school were left defenseless when Belgian peacekeepers withdrew on orders. Interahamwe attacked immediately.

name: Nyarubuye Church massacre
desc: One of the largest single massacre sites. Approximately 20,000 people gathered at Nyarubuye parish seeking safety. Over several days, militia and soldiers killed nearly everyone.

name: Nyamata Church massacre
desc: Approximately 10,000 sheltering in and around Nyamata Church were killed over several days. Today it stands as a genocide memorial with victims' remains preserved inside.

name: Ntarama Church massacre
desc: Around 5,000 Tutsi gathered at Ntarama Church seeking protection were massacred. The church has been preserved as a memorial with victims' belongings on display.

name: Kibungo area killings
desc: Across Kibungo prefecture in the Eastern Province, systematic killings targeted Tutsi communities at multiple gathering points including churches and schools.

name: Rwamagana district massacres
desc: In Rwamagana district, Tutsi populations were rounded up and killed at churches, government buildings, and roadblocks across the district.

name: Byumba — Northern killings begin
desc: In Byumba prefecture, killings began as militia targeted Tutsi communities. The RPF advance from Uganda also passed through this area, complicating the security situation.

name: Ruhengeri killings
desc: In Ruhengeri, killings were organized by local authorities and militia. The mountainous terrain of the Virunga area provided some refuge but systematic searches continued.

name: Kinigi area violence
desc: Near Kinigi at the base of the Virunga volcanoes, Tutsi communities were attacked by organized militia despite the remote, mountainous terrain.

name: Gashaki — Northern Province
desc: In the Gashaki sector, militia organized roadblocks and conducted house-to-house killings of Tutsi families across the rural hills.

name: Sainte-Famille Church, Kigali
desc: Thousands sought refuge at Sainte-Famille Church in central Kigali. Militia regularly entered to select and remove people for killing throughout the genocide.

name: Gatwaro Stadium, Kibuye
desc: Authorities directed Tutsi to gather at Gatwaro Stadium claiming protection. Over several days, an estimated 12,000 were killed in coordinated attacks.

name: Kibuye Church massacres
desc: Across Kibuye prefecture, systematic killings at churches and public buildings. The prefecture suffered some of the highest casualty rates proportional to its Tutsi population.

name: Gisenyi killings begin
desc: In Gisenyi at the northern shore of Lake Kivu, organized killing of Tutsi civilians began under direction of local military and administrative authorities.

name: Bisesero resistance begins
desc: The Tutsi community of Bisesero organized defense of the hills. For two months, survivors fought back with stones and traditional weapons against constant militia and military attacks.

name: Butare — Prefect replaced, killings begin
desc: Butare was the last prefecture where large-scale killing began. The Tutsi prefect was removed and killed. Within days, Butare experienced the most intense violence.

name: Murambi Technical School
desc: Authorities directed ~50,000 Tutsi to Murambi Technical School, promising protection. Water and food were cut off. On the night of April 21, soldiers and militia killed an estimated 45,000 in a single night.

name: Gikongoro massacres
desc: Across Gikongoro prefecture, one of the most affected areas, killings continued at multiple sites. The mountainous terrain was systematically searched.

name: Nyanza — Southern Province
desc: In Nyanza, the former royal capital, organized massacres targeted Tutsi populations at churches, schools, and administrative buildings.

name: Gitarama/Muhanga massacres
desc: The interim government temporarily relocated to Gitarama. The presence of extremist officials accelerated killings across the region.

name: Cyangugu — southwestern killings
desc: In Cyangugu along the DRC border, systematic killings targeted Tutsi communities near Lake Kivu. Many victims' bodies were disposed of in the lake.

name: Nyamirambo, Kigali
desc: In densely populated Nyamirambo, house-to-house searches continued throughout the genocide. The mixed neighborhood meant hiding places were systematically uncovered.

name: Rilima — Eastern Province
desc: In the Rilima area south of Kigali, Tutsi communities were attacked at churches and schools where they had gathered for protection.

name: Rusumo border area
desc: Near the Rusumo border crossing with Tanzania, killings targeted communities. Bodies were disposed of in the Kagera River, flowing into Lake Victoria.

name: Bisesero — final assault
desc: French soldiers discovered ~1,500 survivors from an original community of 50,000+. Despite earlier contact, the promised return was delayed. The total death toll at Bisesero exceeded 50,000.

name: Operation Turquoise launched
desc: France launched a UN-authorized intervention establishing a humanitarian zone in SW Rwanda. It saved some lives but has been criticized for enabling perpetrators' escape into Zaire.

name: RPF advances on Kigali
desc: The RPF continued military advance from the northeast toward Kigali, gradually encircling the capital as the international community failed to intervene.

name: RPF captures Kigali
desc: The RPF captured Kigali, effectively ending the genocide in the capital. Surviving residents emerged from hiding.

name: RPF captures Butare
desc: RPF forces took Butare, ending organized mass killing in the south. An enormous proportion of the Tutsi population had been murdered.

name: RPF captures Gisenyi — genocide ends
desc: The RPF captured the last stronghold in Gisenyi near the DRC border, marking the effective end of the genocide. Over 100 days, approximately 1,000,000 Tutsi and moderate Hutu were killed.

name: Kigali Memorial — Gisozi
desc: Final resting place for over 250,000 victims. Established in 2004 as a place of remembrance and education documenting the genocide's history, causes, and consequences.

name: Colline Ibanda — Western hills
desc: In the Western hills near Colline Ibanda, militia conducted systematic searches of the forested terrain, attacking Tutsi families who had fled into the hillsides.

name: Kabarore — Eastern
desc: Massacres in the Kabarore area of the Eastern Province targeted communities at gathering points and along major roads.

name: Birambo — Western interior
desc: In the interior hills of the Western Province near Birambo, systematic killings continued as militia received reinforcements from Kigali.
